title: The Making of Claude Code
locator: Feature page and newsroom entry: oral history from internal CLI to public coding agent
claim_supported: Anthropic documents Claude Code's path from internal CLI experiments and early team use to an external product.
limitation: First-party retrospective with survivorship bias; it does not establish a universal product-development rule.

title: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5
locator: Lines 11-40 and 168-215: launch, safeguards, access, and availability
claim_supported: Anthropic launched Fable 5 and Mythos 5 on June 9 with differentiated safeguards and access conditions.
limitation: Capability and safeguard performance descriptions are Anthropic's own claims.

title: Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
locator: Lines 11-22: directive, suspension effect, and Anthropic's account of the reported bypass
claim_supported: Anthropic suspended access to both models on June 12 because it could not reliably enforce the directive's nationality restriction in real time.
limitation: The directive and jailbreak are described from Anthropic's position; the underlying report is not public.

title: Redeploying Fable 5
locator: Lines 17-33: restored access, event sequence, and proposed shared jailbreak framework
claim_supported: Anthropic restored global Fable 5 access on July 1 and proposed a shared process for triaging and communicating jailbreak severity.
limitation: The event interpretation and proposed framework are first-party; restoration conditions vary by product surface.

title: More details on Fable 5's cyber safeguards and our jailbreak framework
locator: Lines 11-25: draft severity framework, disclosure channel, and scope of cyber safeguards
claim_supported: Anthropic published an early draft jailbreak severity framework and opened a HackerOne reporting channel.
limitation: The framework is explicitly an early draft rather than an agreed industry standard.

title: Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security
locator: Lines 181-184: pre-release government access and selection of trusted partners
claim_supported: The executive order established a government role in early access and trusted-partner coordination for covered frontier models.
limitation: The order defines a governance mechanism; it does not prove operational effectiveness.

title: White House Launches Gold Eagle Initiative for Unprecedented Cybersecurity Vulnerability Coordination
locator: Lines 150-166: launch, coordination model, intake, prioritization, and scanning verification
claim_supported: The White House announced GOLD EAGLE on July 14 as a coordination mechanism for vulnerability intake, verification, prioritization, and remediation.
limitation: Operational impact and speed claims are not independently demonstrated in the release.

title: Code scanning shows AI security detections on pull requests
locator: Lines 41-53: PR integration, AI label, informational status, enablement, preview availability, and billing
claim_supported: GitHub released non-blocking AI security findings inside pull requests as a public preview with specific enterprise, CodeQL, Copilot, and credit requirements.
limitation: Public preview is enterprise-oriented and GitHub provides no independent accuracy or false-positive evaluation.

title: GitHub Models is being fully retired on July 30, 2026
locator: Lines 39-46: full retirement scope, affected customers, brownout dates, and suggested alternatives
claim_supported: GitHub Models will retire for all customers on July 30, with brownouts on July 16 and July 23 affecting the playground, catalog, inference API, BYOK endpoints, and UI.
limitation: The service remains time-limited until retirement; the first brownout is past and the next dates require a final freshness check before publishing.
